dimension_results.raw_response:
ISSUE_1_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified that the telecommunications receipt is missing the required ICP company details (Global People s.r.l.). This is a mandatory requirement for non-travel expenses as clearly stated in the knowledge base: "Invoice must show Global People s.r.l., Via Venti Settembre 3, Torino (TO) CAP 10121, Italy VAT: IT12455930011 C.F: 12455930011 - The Local Employers name and details should appear on invoices, not the workers."

ISSUE_2_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified the requirement for traceable payment methods under Italian law, specifically citing "Article 10 of Budget Law 2025" and listing the acceptable payment methods. This aligns perfectly with the knowledge base which states: "Under Article 10 of Budget Law 2025, obligation to use traceable payment methods: Bank transfers, Postal transfers, Credit cards, Debit cards, Prepaid cards, Bank or cashier's checks."

ISSUE_3_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified that telecommunications expenses may be subject to gross-up based on tax policies. This aligns with the knowledge base rule: "All approved expenses will be paid as NET to the employee and grossed up if they are not tax-free." The AI appropriately recommended reviewing specific gross-up guidelines for telecommunications expenses.

ISSUE_4_VALIDATION_SCORE: 100 - The AI correctly identified that receipts should include clear exchange rate information when transactions are in foreign currency, citing the knowledge base rule: "Receipts should be submitted with the same currency and clear exchange rate." While the receipt appears to be in EUR (local currency), the AI is correctly applying the general rule about exchange rate transparency.

CONFIDENCE: 100
